---
title: "Research-Ops Platform"
subtitle: "From a .STATUS file to a live research board"
---

A small toolchain that turns each project's `.STATUS` file into a **queryable registry** and a **live research
board** in Obsidian, with a contract that keeps every project well-formed.

## Pipeline

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    S[".STATUS<br/>(per project)"] -->|"atlas sync --research"| A["atlas registry<br/>+ atlas doctor"]
    A --> L["project list --kind / --json"]
    A --> M["MCP atlas_get_projects"]
    M -->|"obs research board"| V["Obsidian vault<br/>research board"]
```

## Tools

| Tool | Role |
|------|------|
| [**atlas**](https://github.com/Data-Wise/atlas) | Registry engine + auditor — `atlas doctor`, `project list --kind`, research-safe sync |
| [**obsidian-cli-ops**](https://github.com/Data-Wise/obsidian-cli-ops) | Renders a deterministic research board from the atlas registry into the vault |
| [**docs-standards**](https://github.com/Data-Wise/docs-standards) | The Project Settings Contract + architecture decision records |

## How it works

- **Tag** a manuscript or program in its `.STATUS` (`kind:`, `target:`/`venue:`, and a `tasks:` block for programs).
- **Sync** — `atlas sync --research` parses it into the registry; `atlas doctor` audits the settings contract
  (`.STATUS` / `CLAUDE.md` / `.obs/sync.yml`) across every project.
- **Render** — `obs research board` draws manuscripts, programs, venues, deadlines, and next-actions into an
  Obsidian board, refreshed deterministically from the registry.

## Learn more

- atlas documentation: <https://data-wise.github.io/atlas/> (registry, cookbook, architecture, MCP)
- [Research-ops overview](https://github.com/Data-Wise/docs-standards/blob/main/research-ops/overview.md) —
  cross-tool ownership map and ADRs.

*Open source. Built for an ADHD-friendly, reproducible research workflow.*
